speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: This yo-yoing between, I'm not worried and oh my God, we need to panic today ... is a hallmark of immature decision making ... we can't ignore this. We can't be complacent ... we have to respond rationally.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: In order to fund the very expensive creation of these models, it needs to be a company, it needs to have a business model. Does the business model get in the way of the values?

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: Many of those are basically fall under the banner of enterprise ... cure diseases ... make energy cheaper and more efficient ... help with education ... increase economic growth.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: Model quality is the most important thing ... Anthropic has never relied on ... this is sticky and people won't switch. I think you wanna have a better model. You want to have a better product.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: The ability to quickly write software ... I definitely think that's going away. If your moat is, we wrote this complex software that no one else can write ... you're not gonna be able to defend that.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: Folks have customer relationships ... know how of how the field works. Folks have unique domain knowledge ... some [moats] are going to become relatively more important. Because there are limiting factors and there may also be new moats.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: I would guess that the software industry gets larger, not smaller. Although there will be some big losers ... the existing incumbents may be smaller in relative terms ... may even go out of business if they don't adapt.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: In a year you'll have three times as much compute ... these compute ramps are very fast ... raising money is kind of the buffer against this cone of uncertainty.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: We were planning for a 10X a year growth in compute ... We saw a greater than three x growth in revenue quarterly ... we're in a locally extreme explosion of compute that's not gonna continue.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: We have a unified company. We have a unified culture ... cultural and organizational unity ... is the biggest factor. And ... the second biggest factor is Claude itself.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: I'm a little worried about that. And in fact, that's half the reason I write myself ... it is just as much to clarify my own thinking ... I think if we just used it end to end ... I would exactly lose that benefit.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: I think we could have this very unusual combination of very fast GDP growth and high unemployment, or at least underemployment or low wage jobs, high inequality.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: AI is making people more productive. But that's the usual hump ... You automate 90% of the job, great, people are 10 times more productive in the other 10% ... But eventually it gets close to a hundred percent.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: AI makes the software engineers more productive, even though AI writes all the code or almost all the code ... [but] it may be better for the AI to just do the thing ... [while] applied AI solutions architect [mixes] technical work and talking to customers.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: There's gonna be a lot of potential for AI to first make people more productive ... then there's gonna be a wholesale AI can do the job ... We always, when we can, try to push [customers] to doing more with the same amount of resources.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: What we've seen here is Claude assists, but a human makes the final call ... Imagine ... the AI model just makes the decision and the human never sees it. That's what we were standing up for.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: The models had been climbing in their ability to find vulnerabilities and importantly turn those vulnerabilities into exploits ... we saw this huge jump ... early companies ... were basically asking us not to release it.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: These classifiers can be jailbroken or gone around ... our own testing ... suggests that these defenses are not strong yet ... we're waiting for getting the defenses to the point where we really have confidence in them.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: The reason that we're giving Mythos to defenders before we give it to attackers is to patch all the bugs ... hopefully six months or a year from now, we have a much more secure internet ecosystem.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: There need to be checks and balances on the power of the AI companies ... the companies need to provide checks on government, and the government needs to provide checks on companies ... required pre-release testing and auditing of the models.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: There's really a premium to how intelligent the models are ... the 10x per year model exponential really breaks [previous] product heuristics ... the value is found on the frontier.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: I don't think it's a moment in time. I think it's a continuous process. We're already seeing it ... AI is able to suggest architectures for the next AI ... What we have is an accelerating exponential.

speaker: Dario Amodei
quote: Yo-yoing between those extreme reactions is incredibly unhelpful ... The right response ... is to say, we're not gonna panic. Our countermeasures will smoothly ratchet up with the power of the technology.
